subject: [PATCH] Documentation/bpf: make it clear that kfuncs should be non-static

reviews.author: Randy Dunlap
reviews.summary: Reviewer Randy Dunlap pointed out a minor typo in the documentation text, suggesting that 'Ofcourse' should be corrected to 'Of course'.
reviews.raw_body:
Not part of this patch, but "Ofcourse," should be "Of course,"
of course.

-- 
~Randy

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch updates the documentation of kernel functions (kfuncs) in BPF to make it clear that they should not be declared as static. The __bpf_kfunc macro prevents issues such as inlining or elision, and kfuncs must have externally visible names for BTF resolution. The patch also notes that sparse may warn about unreferenced kfuncs, but these warnings can be ignored.
